title: Recursive type has infinite size
explanation: This error occurs when a type directly or indirectly contains itself, making its size infinite and impossible to allocate on the stack. This commonly happens with tree or list data structures.
error_message: error[E0072]: recursive type `List` has infinite size

fixes.description: Use Box for recursive struct fields

tip: Box<T> is a heap-allocated pointer with a known size. Wrap the recursive field in Box, Rc, or Arc to break the infinite size cycle.
